cards.title: Major Tech Layoffs in 2026 Cite AI as Factor
cards.body: TechCrunch compiled a running list of significant layoffs announced by major tech companies throughout 2026. AI is cited as a stated factor in these workforce reductions.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Open Source Bug-Finding Initiative
cards.body: OpenAI is leveraging AI to help the open source community identify and patch vulnerabilities. The initiative aims to strengthen security across open source projects.

cards.title: Agentic AI Gets 'Loopy' with Autonomous Swarms
cards.body: A new development called 'the loop' extends agentic AI by enabling swarms of agents to work continuously in the background. These autonomous systems operate endlessly without human intervention.

cards.title: Groq Raises $650M After Nvidia Not-Acqui-Hire Deal
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq confirmed a $650 million funding round and is now re-staffing following Nvidia's $20 billion not-acqui-hire deal. The company is doubling down on its neocloud business.

cards.title: Nvidia Cuts Data Center Water Use, Misses Bigger Problem
cards.body: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that reduces water consumption inside data centers. However, critics note it doesn't address AI's largest water use: fossil fuel power plants.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Invests $75M in Hollywood AI Films
cards.body: Google DeepMind and A24 partnered to build AI filmmaking tools with a $75 million investment. The collaboration aims to revolutionize content creation in the entertainment industry.

cards.title: Amazon Tests Alexa+ Hindi Version in India
cards.body: Amazon is expanding its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India with Hindi-language support. The company is inviting Indian users to test the localized version.

cards.title: Reflection AI Signs $150M Monthly SpaceX Compute Deal
cards.body: Open source AI lab Reflection AI will pay $150 million monthly for immediate access to Nvidia's GB300 chips through SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis. The deal runs through 2029.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper is departing Google DeepMind to join rival Anthropic. He's one of several high-profile researchers recently leaving DeepMind.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Your Friends
cards.body: Signal President Meredith Whittaker emphasized that AI chatbots are not conscious beings or sentient entities. She warned users against treating these systems as actual friends or confidants.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its latest models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails.

cards.title: Anthropic Ban Numbers Show Continued Market Traction
cards.body: Despite the US government's ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, market metrics indicate continued brand strength. The regulatory action hasn't suppressed user interest in the company.

cards.title: Export Controls on AI May Prove Ineffective Again
cards.body: Cybersecurity experts note that 30 years of export controls on security-related software have failed to stop proliferation. Questions remain about whether similar restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos will succeed.

cards.title: Billionaire Ambani Weaves AI Into Telecom Services
cards.body: Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services used by over 500 million people. The conglomerate aims to embed AI into calls, apps, and homes at scale.

cards.title: Allbirds CEO Launches AI Startup With Big Seed Round
cards.body: The CEO of Allbirds is launching a new AI-focused business backed by substantial seed funding. The startup still needs to assemble a full team to execute its vision.

cards.title: US Questions If ASML Chip Tool Reached China
cards.body: The US government suggested that ASML's advanced chip manufacturing tool may have reached China. However, commercial interests make such a scenario seem unlikely for ASML.

cards.title: Apple's iOS 27 Brings Practical AI Features Beyond Siri
cards.body: Apple announced an AI overhaul for Siri at WWDC, but iOS 27 includes useful AI features across the entire system. Many practical improvements extend beyond the assistant itself.

cards.title: In the Weights: New AI-Centric Vanity Search Tool
cards.body: A new service called In the Weights offers AI-centric vanity search capabilities. Users can check their 'In the Weights score' to measure online AI presence.

cards.title: Trump Administration Cracks Down on Anthropic
cards.body: The Trump administration has taken new regulatory actions against AI company Anthropic. The episode raises questions about what prompted these moves and their broader impact.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Open Source Bug Patching Initiative
cards.body: OpenAI is leveraging AI to help the open source community identify and patch security vulnerabilities. The initiative aims to strengthen ecosystem-wide security.

cards.title: Agentic AI Takes Next Step With Continuous 'Loops'
cards.body: AI swarms can now work endlessly in the background, authorizing multiple agents to operate continuously. This represents a major advancement in agentic AI capabilities.

cards.title: Groq Confirms $650M Raise After Nvidia Deal
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq raised significant funding and is hiring new executives following Nvidia's not-acqui-hire deal. The company is doubling down on its neocloud business.

cards.title: Nvidia's Water Efficiency Doesn't Address AI's Core Problem
cards.body: Nvidia announced new cooling systems reducing data center water use, but this ignores AI's biggest water consumption from fossil fuel power plants. The solution addresses only part of the sustainability challenge.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Invests $75M in AI Filmmaking Tools
cards.body: Google DeepMind and A24 are partnering to develop AI tools for Hollywood filmmaking. The collaboration signals major industry momentum for AI in creative production.

cards.title: Amazon Expands Conversational AI Alexa+ to India
cards.body: Amazon is testing its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ in India with Hindi-language support. The rollout aims to increase the assistant's global footprint.

cards.title: SpaceX Inks $150M Monthly Compute Deal With AI Lab
cards.body: Reflection AI will pay SpaceX $150 million monthly through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips. The deal highlights surging demand for AI compute infrastructure.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: John Jumper, a Nobel laureate, is departing Google DeepMind to join rival AI company Anthropic. The high-profile departure signals ongoing talent competition in AI.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker Warns AI Chatbots Aren't Your Friends
cards.body: Signal executive Meredith Whittaker emphasizes that AI chatbots are tools, not conscious beings or friends. She's pushing back against anthropomorphizing language around AI systems.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails.

cards.title: Anthropic Faces Export Controls on New AI Models
cards.body: The Trump administration cracked down on Anthropic following cybersecurity model developments. The enforcement raises questions about how such policies affect the broader AI ecosystem.

cards.title: Apple Reveals Practical AI Features Coming to iOS 27
cards.body: Beyond Siri's AI overhaul, iOS 27 includes numerous practical AI features across the system. Apple is making AI integration a central focus of its platform update.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into Services for 500M Users
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI into telecom services reaching over 500 million people. The rollout demonstrates AI adoption at massive scale in developing markets.

cards.title: Startup Claims Breakthrough in LLM Processing Bottleneck
cards.body: Subquadratic emerged from stealth claiming to solve a mathematical bottleneck constraining large language models for nearly a decade. The claim generated skepticism but represents potential major progress in LLM efficiency.

cards.title: Brain-Computer Interface Trials Show Real Progress
cards.body: Casey Harrell, a man with ALS, has become the first 'power user' of a brain implant after nearly three years of use. The breakthrough demonstrates practical AI applications in assistive technology.

cards.title: How Anthropic Talked Itself Into Export Ban
cards.body: Anthropic's public disclosures about its AI capabilities may have inadvertently prompted government export controls. The situation highlights tensions between transparency and regulatory scrutiny.
